items.title: The National | Spring blizzard traps hundreds of drivers in Alberta
items.summary_auto: April 24, 2026 | A spring blizzard traps hundreds of drivers in their cars on an impassable highway in northern Alberta. OpenAI CEO Sam Altman apologizes to Tumbler Ridge, B.C. And a Vancouver toddler's incredible recovery after being severely injured in last year's deadly Lapu Lapu Day attack.

items.title: Deportation of truck driver responsible for Humboldt Broncos bus crash deferred temporarily
Fact check: Trump and Iran share false information about 8 Iranian women
items.summary_auto: Did U.S. President Donald Trump save eight women from being executed in Iran? Did he share AI-generated images of eight Iranian women? CBC’s Fact Check team breaks down what we know.

items.title: Could nuclear power solve the Yukon’s energy problem?
items.summary_auto: Officials from Ontario and the Yukon have entered into a partnership that marks “the first step” toward deploying small modular reactors in the Yukon – technology they say could help solve the territory’s energy crisis.

items.title: Yes, a Steak Canadian is a real thing. No, it wasn't invented 'in a place called Ontario'
items.summary_auto: Despite its name, the sandwich, which recently went viral on YouTube, is a purely British phenomenon.

items.title: Rob Reiner's son Jake describes 'living nightmare' after parents' murder
items.summary_auto: Jake Reiner, the eldest son of Rob and Michelle Reiner, published a ‌tribute to his late parents on Friday and described the "living nightmare" he has experienced since their murders in December.

items.title: Days of time change 'pretty much at the end' in Manitoba, premier suggests
items.summary_auto: Manitoba's premier is moving the needle a tick closer toward ditching the spring forward, fall back ritual, in a move that could usher in a "Prairie time zone."

items.title: OpenAI's Sam Altman writes apology to community of Tumbler Ridge
Stuck on the highway: Heavy snow, high winds pummel parts of Alberta
Trump dispatches U.S. envoys to Pakistan for new talks with Iran's foreign minister
items.summary_auto: U.S. President Donald Trump is sending his envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner to Pakistan to meet with Iran's foreign minister, the White House said Friday, as officials in the South Asian nation pushed to revive ceasefire talks between the U.S. and Iran.

items.title: Alexandre Boulerice, NDP's sole Quebec MP, to leave party and run provincially: sources
items.summary_auto: Alexandre Boulerice is expected to announce that he is leaving the federal NDP and will run provincially for Québec Solidaire, according to Radio-Canada sources.

items.title: Gatineau woman told her bird feeder could lead to $400 fine
items.summary_auto: A Gatineau woman said she's taken aback after the city warned her she could face a $400 fine over the bird feeder in her backyard.

items.title: 2-time French Open champ Alcaraz won't defend title after further testing on injured wrist
items.summary_auto: Two-time defending champion Carlos Alcaraz won't attempt to defend his French Open title due to a right wrist injury.

items.title: Timber Timbre frontman Taylor Kirk, dead at 44
items.summary_auto: Taylor Kirk, the singer-songwriter behind the band Timber Timbre, has died at 44. A representative for Kirk confirmed to CBC Music that the singer died suddenly on April 14.

items.title: City fire crews responding to multiple wind-related incidents as powerful winds whip city
items.summary_auto: The Calgary Fire Department says it has responded to multiple wind-related incidents over the last two days as strong gusts whipping through the city have created havoc for some businesses.

items.title: Winnipeg man previously accused of threatening PM now charged with advocating genocide, promotion of hatred
items.summary_auto: A Winnipeg man arrested earlier this year who allegedly posted threats against Jews and Muslims, and against Prime Minister Mark Carney, has now been charged with advocating genocide and wilful promotion of hatred.

items.title: Austrian man finds out his grandfather was a Nazi in just a few clicks. He's encouraging others to do the same
items.summary_auto: Thanks to a new online tool from the German newspaper Die Zeit, it only took Christian Rainer a few seconds to confirm what he'd long suspected — his grandfather was a card-carrying member of the Nazi Party.

items.title: 2 killed in Israeli strike on south Lebanon despite ceasefire, health officials say
items.summary_auto: An Israeli airstrike killed two people in the southern village of Touline on Friday, Lebanon's Health Ministry said, as Israel's army ordered residents to evacuate another southern Lebanese town, a day after Israel and Lebanon extended their ceasefire by three weeks.

items.title: Justin Trudeau says U.S. economic coercion risks pushing Canada closer to China
items.summary_auto: Former prime minister Justin Trudeau is warning that American tariffs threaten to drive Canada closer to China in the auto sector.

items.title: Ottawa approves Enbridge's $4B Sunrise natural gas pipeline expansion project
Criminal probe into Federal Reserve chairman Jerome Powell is ending, U.S. Justice Dept. says
items.summary_auto: The Justice Department is ‌closing its investigation ​into cost overruns ​in renovations at the ​Federal Reserve under chairman ‌Jerome Powell, U.S. Attorney Jeanine Pirro said on Friday.

items.title: South Korean prosecutors deny arrest warrant request for K-pop mogul behind BTS
items.summary_auto: South Korean prosecutors on Friday rejected a police request for an arrest warrant for music tycoon Bang Si-Hyuk — the chairman of the agency behind the K-pop supergroup BTS.

items.title: Ontario couple in months-long murder trial involving boys in their care to learn their fates in May
items.summary_auto: A judge's decision in the murder trial for Becky Hamber and Brandy Cooney, who are accused in the 2022 death of a 12-year-old and alleged torture of his younger brother, will be handed down May 5 as part of proceedings that began in mid-September.

items.title: LNG Canada ordered to identify cause of black smoke during flaring at Kitimat facility
items.summary_auto: The B.C. Energy Regulator has issued an order to LNG Canada to address at least two instances of black smoke emissions during flaring at the site in Kitimat, B.C.

items.title: Ticketmaster delists Ontario resale tickets after new law caps prices
items.summary_auto: Ticketmaster has begun delisting resale tickets for Ontario events to comply with a new provincial law that caps the price of such tickets at face value.

items.title: Ex-neurologist not guilty on 48 counts of sexual assault, Ontario judge rules
How the Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples Act drove the B.C. government to a crisis point
items.summary_auto: When governments sign a document committing to obtain the free and informed consent of Indigenous peoples before approving projects affecting their lands or resources, do they mean it? That is the question at the heart of the controversy that has plagued the B.C. government for months.

items.title: Syrian authorities arrest suspected leader of notorious Tadamon massacre
items.summary_auto: Syrian authorities said Friday they have arrested a former intelligence officer who appeared in a video leaked four years ago that purportedly showed him and his comrades fatally shooting dozens of people during the country's conflict.

items.title: Meta to cut 8,000 jobs, Microsoft to offer voluntary buyouts for thousands
items.summary_auto: Meta is laying off about 8,000 workers, or about 10 per cent of its workforce, the company said Thursday as it continues to ramp up spending on artificial intelligence infrastructure and highly paid AI-expert hires.

items.title: Halifax Tides player collaborates with N.S. reggae artist to create women’s soccer league anthem
items.summary_auto: The new anthem for Canada’s professional women’s soccer league features a Halifax Tides FC player whose high-energy collaboration with a well-known reggae singer will be the soundtrack to the 2026 Northern Super League season.

items.title: N.B. child still waiting for dental surgery after two years
items.summary_auto: New Brunswick's only board-certified pediatric dentist says a lack of operating-room time has pushed his waiting list to more than 300 children, and some of them wait up to three years for surgery.

items.title: Canada’s top general tries to reassure Ukraine as NATO tensions flare
Indigenous ancestral remains were found 20 years ago. Ontario says new homeowners have to investigate
Mounties, border officers and cyber spies shut out of early retirement incentive
items.summary_auto: Front-line security and intelligence workers including Mounties won't be able to access the government's penalty‑free early retirement incentive as federal public safety agencies focus on getting more people in the door — not out.

items.title: Why Trump's appearance at White House correspondents dinner is triggering controversy
items.summary_auto: U.S. President Donald Trump, who regularly attacks news coverage and journalists he dislikes with lawsuits and verbal tirades, will be the featured guest at Saturday's White House Correspondents' Association dinner, an annual gala celebrating freedom of the press.

items.title: Family says mom of baby who died was ‘begging for help’ at remote Ontario hospital, waiting for doctor's call
items.summary_auto: The family of an 11-month-old Cree baby from Attawapiskat who died in a Fort Albany hospital in northeastern Ontario this month says he would still be alive if he had received the health care he needed and deserved. Ontario’s Office of the Chief Coroner says it's investigating.

items.title: Michael Jackson biopic turns up the music and mutes the controversy. What's wrong with that?
items.summary_auto: Antoine Fuqua's Michael is a surface-level replay of the iconic King of Pop's life. Audiences get toe-tapping entertainment value and an impressive impersonation by nephew Jaafar Jackson at the expense of virtually any investigation into the mind and motivations of the eponymous star.

items.title: Saros is an exhilarating sci-fi shooter set in a haunting, cryptic world
items.summary_auto: Finnish studio Housemarque follows up from its 2021 hit Returnal with Saros, refining the "bullet ballet" action to make it a more approachable game without sacrificing its adrenaline-pumping nature.
